title: Boolean Evaluation
description: Given a boolean expression consisting of the symbols `0` (false), `1` (true), `&` (AND), `|` (OR), and `^` (XOR), and a desired result value `result`, implement a function to count the number of ways of parenthesizing the expression such that it evaluates to `result`.

content.text: Given a boolean expression consisting of the symbols `0` (false), `1` (true), `&` (AND), `|` (OR), and `^` (XOR), and a desired result value `result`, implement a function to count the number of ways of parenthesizing the expression such that it evaluates to `result`.

Given a boolean expression consisting of the symbols `0` (false), `1` (true), `&` (AND), `|` (OR), and `^` (XOR), and a desired result value `result`, implement a function to count the number of ways of parenthesizing the expression such that it evaluates to `result`. The answer may overflow, return it mod 1000000007. **Example 1:** ``` Input: s = "1^0|0|1", result = 0 Output: 2 ``` **Example 2:** ``` Input: s = "0&0&0&1^1|0", result = 1 Output: 10 ```
